name: command-development
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create a slash command", "add a command", "write a custom command", "define command arguments", "use command frontmatter", "organize commands", "create command with file references", "interactive command", "use AskUserQuestion in command", or needs guidance on slash command structure, YAML frontmatter fields, dynamic arguments, bash execution in commands, user interaction patterns, or command development best practices for Claude Code.
capabilities: Create slash commands as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter configuration
Define dynamic arguments using $ARGUMENTS, $1, $2 placeholders for parameterized commands
Include file contents in commands using @ syntax file references
Execute bash commands inline with !` backtick syntax for dynamic context gathering
Organize commands with namespacing and subdirectories for scalable command libraries
Integrate commands with plugin agents, skills, and hooks for complex workflows
author: anthropics
securitySummary: Official Anthropic documentation skill containing only markdown teaching content and command examples. No executable code, network operations, or filesystem modifications. Contains comprehensive guidance on slash command development patterns for Claude Code.
valueStatement: Creating custom slash commands requires understanding file format, YAML configuration, and dynamic features. This skill provides comprehensive guidance on building reusable, powerful commands with arguments, file references, and bash execution for Claude Code.
userTitle: Create slash commands for Claude Code
limitations: Commands are written as instructions for Claude consumption, not as user-facing messages
Plugin-specific features like CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT require plugin context to function
Commands cannot modify themselves or other command files at runtime
Complex multi-step workflows may require multiple coordinated commands

useCases.title: Create project-specific commands
useCases.description: Define reusable workflows for code review, testing, and deployment that team members can access via slash command invocation.

useCases.title: Bundle commands with plugins
useCases.description: Package plugin-specific commands that use CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT for portable paths and integrate with plugin agents and skills.

useCases.title: Build interactive commands
useCases.description: Create commands that use AskUserQuestion to gather user input through structured multi-step workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Basic command creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new slash command called /review-code that reviews files for quality issues. Include description and allowed-tools for Read access.

promptTemplates.title: Command with arguments
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a slash command that accepts a PR number as argument. Use $1 placeholder and include argument-hint in frontmatter.

promptTemplates.title: File references
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a command that uses @ syntax to reference configuration files. Show how to load multiple files in one command.

promptTemplates.title: Plugin command
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a plugin command that uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} to reference scripts and templates in the plugin directory.

bestPractices: Write commands as instructions TO Claude, not messages TO users. Use imperative form like Review this code for issues.
Use the most restrictive allowed-tools setting. For read-only analysis, specify Read and Grep rather than granting all tool access.
Document arguments with argument-hint field. Use descriptive names like [pr-number] instead of generic placeholders.
antiPatterns: Writing commands as user-facing messages such as This command will review your code instead of direct instructions like Review this code for issues.
Using overly broad allowed-tools settings like Bash(*) when Bash(git:*) would suffice and be more secure
Omitting argument-hint documentation, which makes commands harder to discover and use correctly

faq.question: What file format do slash commands use?
faq.answer: Slash commands are Markdown files with .md extension stored in .claude/commands/, ~/.claude/commands/, or plugin-name/commands/ directories.

faq.question: How do I pass arguments to a command?
faq.answer: Use $1, $2, $3 for positional arguments or $ARGUMENTS for all arguments as a single string. Define argument-hint in frontmatter for user documentation.

faq.question: Can commands execute bash scripts?
faq.answer: Yes, use !`command` syntax to execute bash and include output. Add Bash to allowed-tools and consider command filters like Bash(git:*).

faq.question: Are command contents visible to users?
faq.answer: Command files exist on the system but their content is only visible through Claude. Users invoke commands via slash name and see results, not the command text.

faq.question: Why is my command not appearing in /help?
faq.answer: Verify file is in correct location, has .md extension, contains valid frontmatter, and restart Claude Code after adding new commands.

faq.question: How are plugin commands different from project commands?
faq.answer: Plugin commands use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for portable paths, can be auto-discovered from plugins, and integrate with plugin agents and skills.
